name: Invoice Ninja
researched_as: Invoice Ninja invoicing and payments platform
license: Elastic License / source-available mix; verify current terms before bundling
what: Open-source-first invoicing, quotes, payments, client portal, and expense tracking platform.
solves: Client-facing invoices, quotes, payment links, reminders, and light business-finance workflows without implementing a custom invoicing layer.
users: Small businesses, agencies, and operators who need invoicing and collections without a full ERP.
fit: Good fit if Hylono needs polished invoices/quotes outside pure Stripe receipts. Stronger direct fit than Akaunting for client-facing invoicing and quote workflows.
runtime: Laravel/PHP application with database, mail, payment integrations, background jobs, and client portal.
components: Web app/API, relational database, mailer, payment connectors, cron/jobs, and PDF/document generation.
deploy: Docker and self-host docs are mature. Production needs mail, backups, payment/webhook testing, and PDF/document storage planning.
deps: Database, PHP runtime, mailer, payment provider credentials, cron, and storage.
datastores: Relational database plus generated invoice/document storage.
auth: Local auth, client portal users, API tokens, and payment-provider credentials.
apis: REST API, webhooks, client portal, payment integrations, and invoice/quote generation.
security: Financial documents and customer details require standard finance-app hardening and role separation.
setup_path: Use official self-host docs and test Stripe/payment integration thoroughly before production.
env: APP_URL, DB settings, mailer, app key, payment provider secrets, queue/cron, and storage paths.
persistence: Back up DB and generated documents; validate invoice numbering and legal template retention.
operations: Upgrade cautiously around tax/PDF/payment changes; test reminder schedules and webhook flows.
failure_points: Confusing Stripe as source of truth vs Invoice Ninja as source of truth, and duplicating invoice ownership across tools.
integration: Good fit for quotes, pro forma invoices, recurring invoices, and finance portal workflows. Can integrate with Stripe, n8n, CRM, and Metabase.
verify: Decide whether Hylono needs separate invoicing/quote workflows beyond Stripe and whether Invoice Ninja or Lago should own invoices if both exist.
risk: Moderate. Useful but finance scope must be explicit.
gdpr: Contains customer and financial data; treat as a finance system.
overlap: Preferred over Akaunting for focused invoicing/quotes/payments in this stack.
codex_read_first: Read current Stripe invoicing/payment flows before proposing Invoice Ninja.
codex_best_path: Use Invoice Ninja only for explicit quote/invoice workflows that are not already cleanly solved in Stripe.
codex_never_assume: Never assume finance is comfortable with a second invoice source of truth without reconciliation rules.
